name: com.sony.dtv.calibrationmonitor
label: Calibrationmonitor
description: Screen calibration app https://play.google.com/store/apps/details/Calman%20for%20BRAVIA?id=com.sony.dtv.calibrationmonitor&hl=en_US Useful app, but can be removed if you don't need it.

name: com.sony.dtv.ecodashboard
label: Ecodashboard
description: Used to configure eco friendly settings. Breaks some options in the settings app if removed.

name: com.sony.dtv.livingfit
label: Livingfit
description: Turns your tv into an accessory by showing pictures. This is different to the screensaver and won't affect it. https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sony.dtv.livingfit&hl=en_US

name: com.sony.dtv.notificationcenter
label: Notificationcenter
description: Notification app which reminds you to use apps from Sony's ecosystem. Can be removed safely without breaking other notifications

name: com.sony.dtv.osat.music
label: Music
description: Music app which streams from your files. https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonyericsson.music&hl=en_US Can be removed if you don't need it.

name: com.sony.dtv.promos
label: Promos
description: Sony's promotion app with offers. You need an account to use it

name: com.sony.dtv.recapp
label: Recapp
description: Allows you to view your reminders and timers but not add them? Can be removed safely

name: com.sony.dtv.seeds.iot
label: Iot
description: Allows you to control your TV with smart speaker e.g Alexa: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sony.dtv.seeds.iot&hl=en_US

name: com.sony.dtv.smarthelp
label: Smarthelp
description: App with documentation of how to use a smart TV. Removing it has no effect

name: com.sony.dtv.sonyselect
label: Sonyselect
description: Shows you bunch of popular subscription services and apps and redirects you to the play store. Better to use play store itself

name: com.sony.dtv.timers
label: Timers
description: Sony's clock app. Can be removed safely but on some Android Tv launchers it makes the Timer button not function. Not a deal breaker as the feature is pretty useless.

name: com.sony.dtv.tvx
label: Tvx
description: Handles input settings. HDMI stops working if this is removed.

name: com.sony.nfx.app.sfrc
label: Sfrc
description: Tied to Sony's recommendations and can be safely got rid of, maybe break suggestions/recommendations

name: com.sony.tvsideview.phone
label: Phone
description: Sony's TV remote control app https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sony.tvsideview.phone

name: com.sony.tvsideview.videoph
label: Videoph
description: Video & TV SideView (replaced by https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sony.tvsideview.phone) Lets you use your smartphone or tablet as a TV remote control for the home.

name: com.sonymobile.advancedlogging
label: Advancedlogging
description: Advanced Logging Sends logs to Sony Mobile. These logs contain a wide range of personal information such as unique device IDs, your location, details regarding running applications, and events/input leading up to a crash. Logging is only active for a short time and automatically disabled once logging has been completed. Logs are uploaded when connected to Wi-Fi and automatically deleted when the upload is complete.

name: com.sonymobile.advancedwidget.topcontacts
label: Topcontacts
description: Top Contacts widget It will show pictures of your most frequently used contacts right on your home screen. REMINDER : Widgets are small applications that you can use directly on the window screen. They also function as shortcuts

name: com.sonymobile.android.addoncamera.dual
label: Dual
description: Provides an addon to enable dual camera functionality, safe to remove if you don’t need camera add-ons

name: com.sonymobile.android.addoncamera.soundphoto
label: Soundphoto
description: Sony Sound Photo Lets you record a background sound and take a photo at the same time with the Sound Photo app.

name: com.sonymobile.android.contacts
label: Contacts
description: Sony contacts

name: com.sonymobile.android.contacts.res.overlay_305
description: Overlay for Sony contacts. Overlays are usually themes.

name: com.sonymobile.android.externalkeyboard
label: Externalkeyboard
description: International keyboard layouts Useless if you use a latin keyboard

name: com.sonymobile.android.externalkeyboardjp
label: Externalkeyboardjp
description: Japanese layout for Sony keyboard.

name: com.sonymobile.androidapp.cameraaddon.areffect
label: Areffect
description: Old package for AR Effect (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.androidapp.cameraaddon.areffect) Lets you add AR (Augmented Reality) effects to your pictures and videos.

name: com.sonymobile.anondata
label: Anondata
description: Anonymous Usage Stats (yes just as com.sonyericsson.idd.agent but it's for other phones) Used to send "anonymous" information about how you use your Sony Smartphone to Sony servers. It remains unclear exactly how this information is anonymized.

name: com.sonymobile.apnupdater
label: Apnupdater
description: Automatically updates APN settings if your carrier changes them? I thought that was the role of com.android.carrierconfig APN: https://tamingthedroid.com/what-apn-settings-mean

name: com.sonymobile.apnupdater.res.overlay_305
description: Overlay for APN Updater. Overlays are usually themes.

name: com.sonymobile.aptx.notifier
label: Notifier
description: Aptx Notifier aptX (formerly apt-X) is a family of proprietary audio codec compression algorithms owned by Qualcomm. If you don't mind closed source codec, aptX has lower latency and is less of a drain on your battery than default codec (AAC) This package is used to display a notification when a device using aptX (bluetooth headphone typically) is connected. Its only use is to tell you that you use aptX bluetooth with the connected device.

name: com.sonymobile.assist
label: Assist
description: Xperia Assist (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.assist) Learns how you use your phone.

name: com.sonymobile.assist.persistent
label: Persistent
description: Related to Xperia Assist (see just above) but I don't know its purpose.

name: com.sonymobile.btidd
label: Btidd
description: Bluetooth stack identifier, may be tied to pairing/security. Best to keep enabled

name: com.sonymobile.cameracommon
label: Cameracommon
description: Shared library for Sony’s camera app and add-ons. Removing breaks camera functionality

name: com.sonymobile.cameracommon.wearablebridge
label: Wearablebridge
description: Camera Wearable bridge Lets you take pictures with your phone by using Sony SmartWatch.

name: com.sonymobile.cellbroadcast.notification
label: Notification
description: Cell information Cell broadcast is designed to deliver messages to multiple users in an area. This is notably used by ISP to send Emergency/Government alerts. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_Broadcast https://www.androidcentral.com/amber-alerts-and-android-what-you-need-know I think this package only handles notifications for broadcasts, not the implementation. It seems like broadcast SMS use normal notifications so there is a chance this package provides special notification for Sony SMS app?

name: com.sonymobile.coresettings
label: Coresettings
description: Contains Sony-specific customisations and settings overlays. Needed for Xperia custom features

name: com.sonymobile.coverapp2
label: Coverapp2
description: Style Cover Themes for lockscreen.

name: com.sonymobile.demoappchecker
label: Demoappchecker
description: Demo app checker Lets you enter/exit (in) the demonstration mode. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demo_mode

name: com.sonymobile.deviceconfigtool
label: Deviceconfigtool
description: Configuration agent Seems to do things cloud related but it's unclear. https://knowledge.protektoid.com/apps/com.sonymobile.deviceconfigtool/91e44f1e19b364411776d758ff3b27f703bd4b60c9399c43c124f37d0c30df27

name: com.sonymobile.devicesecurity.service
label: Service
description: DeviceSecurityService Not needed. Collects sim and network data.

name: com.sonymobile.dualshockmanager
label: Dualshockmanager
description: DUALSHOCK Provide PlayStation DualShock controller support for Android (Settings > Device connection > Dualshock)

name: com.sonymobile.email
label: Email
description: Sony Email app

name: com.sonymobile.entrance
label: Entrance
description: What's New (discontinued in 2014) Provided news from Sony products through extremely annoying automated notifications.

name: com.sonymobile.getmore.client
label: Client
description: Xperia Tips (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.getmore.client) Gives you tips for your Xperia device.

name: com.sonymobile.getset
label: Getset
description: Xperia Actions (discontinued) Lets you automate some actions (only a few) https://support.sonymobile.com/global-en/xperiaxz/userguide/xperia-actions/

name: com.sonymobile.getset.priv
label: Priv
description: Xperia Actions System Same thing as com.sonymobile.getset.

name: com.sonymobile.gettoknowit
label: Gettoknowit
description: Introduction to Xperia (discontinued) Introduces you the features of your phone.

name: com.sonymobile.glovemode
label: Glovemode
description: Sony Glove mode Lets you use your smart phone and touch the screen while wearing regular gloves.

name: com.sonymobile.googleanalyticsproxy
label: Googleanalyticsproxy
description: Google Analytics Proxy Allows you to publicly share your Google Analytics reporting data https://developers.google.com/analytics/solutions/google-analytics-super-proxy

name: com.sonymobile.indeviceintelligence
label: Indeviceintelligence
description: Xperia Intelligence Engine This app is supposed to understand how you use the phone, the apps you prefer, and will suggest tips and options based on app usage, how often you use an app, what time of day... For me this just looks like a AI bullshit app who has a huge list of permissions and launch in background at boot This app performs geofencing (check if your are located in a certain perimeter, near your home for instance) and this doesn't looks great privacy-wise (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geo-fence)

name: com.sonymobile.intelligent.backlight
label: Backlight
description: Smart backlight control Keeps the screen on as long as the device is held in your hand. Once you put down the device, the screen turns off according to your sleep setting.

name: com.sonymobile.intelligent.gesture
label: Gesture
description: Smart call handling Lets you handle incoming calls without touching the screen. https://support.sonymobile.com/global-en/xperiaxz/userguide/smart-call-handling/

name: com.sonymobile.intelligent.iengine
label: Iengine
description: According to a Sony user it is part of Smart Screen rotation (auto screen rotation based on the gyroscope). Doesn't seem reliable. Does it break the screen-rotation if removed? On Xperia 10VI: doesn't break rotation.

name: com.sonymobile.intelligent.observer
label: Observer
description: IntelligentObserver ???? (but intelligent stuff are safe to remove)

name: com.sonymobile.lifelog
label: Lifelog
description: Lifelog (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.lifelog) Another activity tracker app.

name: com.sonymobile.moviecreator.rmm
label: Rmm
description: Movie Creator (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.moviecreator.rmm) Automatically creates short movies using your photos and videos.

name: com.sonymobile.mtp.extension.fotaupdate
label: Fotaupdate
description: fota update service FOTA = Firmware Over-The-Air FOTA allows manufacturers to remotely install new software updates, features and services. Given there is "mtp.extension" in the package name, I think it lets you update your phone via your PC. What's weird is that it should be called SEUS then (https://www.mobilefun.co.uk/blog/2008/06/software-updates-sony-ericsson/)

name: com.sonymobile.music.googlelyricsplugin
label: Googlelyricsplugin
description: Google lyrics plugin Provides lyrics from Google in the sony music app

name: com.sonymobile.music.youtubeplugin

description: Sony's PiP (Picture in Picture) Allows videos to shrink down to a small resizable window. Only useful on Android Oreo versions which provide native support for Pip ? https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/ui/picture-in-picture https://support.sonymobile.com/global-en/xperiaxz1compact/faq/apps-&-settings/8019307455ff6184015e92f63324005926/

name: com.sonymobile.pobox
label: Pobox
description: Xperia Japanese keyboard Package is named after POBox (Predictive Operation Based On eXample), a Japanese text entry technology and ambiguous retrieval, proposed in 1998 by Sony CSL fellow Toshiyuki Masuda. https://www.sonycsl.co.jp/project/402/

name: com.sonymobile.prediction
label: Prediction
description: Sony text prediction (for Sony keyboard) It's only a supposition. Can someone confirm ?

name: com.sonymobile.retaildemo

name: com.sonymobile.scan3d
label: Scan3D
description: Sony 3D Creator (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.scan3d) Lets you capture your stuff in 3D, from your smartphone, and turn people and objects into high-resolution 3D avatars. https://www.sonymobile.com/global-en/apps-services/3d-creator/

name: com.sonymobile.simlockunlockapp
label: Simlockunlockapp
description: Sim Lock Provide menu (type *#*#7378423#*#* in dialer) to see if your device is locked to a network carrier It need confirmation because it also could be related to SIM network unlock code.

name: com.sonymobile.smartcharger
label: Smartcharger
description: Battery Care Detects your charging patterns and estimates the start and end time of your regular charging period. The rate of charging is controlled so that your battery reaches 100% just before you disconnect the charger. https://support.sonymobile.com/gb/xperiaxz/userguide/battery-and-power-management/

name: com.sonymobile.support
label: Support
description: Sony Support (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.support) Useless support app.

name: com.sonymobile.synchub
label: Synchub
description: Sony Backup & restore feature to/from Google drive ? Can someone confirm ? Does it impact com.sonyericsson.mtp.extension.backuprestore ? https://support.sonymobile.com/global-en/xperia10/faq/apps-&-settings/801930747866b72a016b307df3b6007faf/

name: com.sonymobile.telephony.extension
label: Extension
description: I have uninstalled it for the user 0 on my phone, and since then I haven't noticed any functionality drop. Cell signal is similar as before, also calls, SMSs and mobile data. I think it is not worth having this package installed because it uses a lot of wakelock time, but I cannot see any cell signal drop since I uninstalled it. In the code found some things: omadm, wifi calling, IMS. Its app for dual SIM reachability? Not sure if its app for debugging.

description: Xperia Transfer Mobile (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sonymobile.xperiatransfermobile) Helps you move your contacts, messages, photos, and much more from your old Android, iOS or Windows Phone device to your new Xperia from Sony.
